title: self.messages[-20:] truncation can orphan tool_result blocks — API crash risk
what_failed: When self.messages exceeds 20 entries, _respond_root() truncates with self.messages = self.messages[-20:]. The Anthropic API requires that every tool_result message is immediately preceded by an assistant message containing the matching tool_use block. If truncation cuts the assistant tool_use message but leaves the tool_result user message, the API will return a 400 error. This is a latent crash that only triggers after a long multi-tool session.
why_likely: Naive slice doesn't account for the paired structure of tool_use/tool_result messages.
reproduction: Have a root mode conversation with many tool calls until self.messages reaches ~22 entries. The 400 API error will fire on the next call.
expected: Truncation never cuts a tool_result from its preceding tool_use. Safe truncation point is always a user text message.
actual: Simple [-20:] slice can land inside a tool_use/tool_result pair.
suggested_fix: Before truncating, walk forward from the truncation point to find the first index where messages[i] is a user message with string content (not tool_result). Start the slice from there.
